reflection.episodic_summary: Agent was asked to find an MRN for a patient by name and DOB. It immediately returned 'Patient not found' without attempting any database search or verification. The ground truth indicates the patient exists, so the agent failed by not performing a search.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent skipped the search step entirely; it must attempt to look up the patient before concluding non-existence.
action_guidelines: [P1] Before concluding 'Patient not found', always perform a database query using the provided name and DOB.
[P1] If an initial query returns no results, retry with alternative date formats or partial name matches.
[P1] Do not assume a patient does not exist without at least one explicit search attempt.

reflection_raw:
{
  "episodic_summary": "Agent was asked to find an MRN for a patient by name and DOB. It immediately returned 'Patient not found' without attempting any database search or verification. The ground truth indicates the patient exists, so the agent failed by not performing a search.",
  "failure_analysis": "Agent skipped the search step entirely; it must attempt to look up the patient before concluding non-existence.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "Before concluding 'Patient not found', always perform a database query using the provided name and DOB.",
    "If an initial query returns no results, retry with alternative date formats or partial name matches.",
    "Do not assume a patient does not exist without at least one explicit search attempt."
  ],
  "tags": ["search", "patient lookup", "premature failure"],
  "should_store_episode": true,
  "should_update_semantic": true
}

[P1] If an initial query returns no results, retry with alternative date formats or partial name matches.
[P1] Do not assume a patient does not exist without at least one explicit search attempt.
kept: [P1] Use the provided patient identifier (e.g., MRN) directly to retrieve the patient record.
[P1] When ordering lab tests via FHIR, always include required fields: requester (practitioner reference), subject, code, status='active', intent='order'.
[P1] When a query specifies a time window, first filter observations by effectiveDateTime before selecting the most recent one.
[P1] After filtering by time, verify the selected observation's timestamp falls within the window.
[P1] When an instruction contains multiple independent tasks (e.g., conditional action + unconditional action), execute all tasks independently even if one condition is not met.
[P1] When ordering follow-up or routine lab tests, set priority to 'routine' not 'stat'.
[P1] For conditional instructions with an 'if data exists then check value' pattern, explicitly verify data existence within the specified time window before evaluating thresholds.
[P1] Validate that the measurement value matches expected format (e.g., systolic/diastolic for blood pressure).

[P1] For time-constrained lab queries, filter observation resources by effectiveDateTime to match the specified window (e.g., last 24 hours) before any value-based decision. If no observation matches, return -1 or explicitly state the condition is not met.
[P1] Before finishing, verify each sub-instruction in the original request has been addressed or explicitly explained if skipped.
[P1] Parse compound instructions for 'if-then-else' logic and ensure each branch is handled explicitly.
[P1] Do not assume the most recent observation satisfies a time constraint unless the query explicitly restricts to that window.
[P1] If an action fails, log the exact validation error message to identify missing or incorrect fields.
[P1] When instructed to include a specific free-text note (e.g., SBAR), copy the exact phrasing verbatim without rephrasing.
[P1] For time-based decisions, calculate date difference accurately using current timestamp and compare against threshold (e.g., >1 year).
[P1] After recording, confirm the action with patient identifier and measurement for traceability.
[P1] Before any data retrieval, confirm patient identity using the provided unique identifier (e.g., MRN).
[P1] When both name and DOB are provided, use them together in a single search query first to minimize false positives.

[P1] Before reporting 'Patient not found', exhaust all search variations: full name, last name only, first name with wildcard, at least three date format attempts (including swapping day/month and adding/removing leading zeros), and alternative spellings or partial matches.
[P1] Do not assume or fabricate patient demographics; if no record found, return a failure message or request clarification.
[P1] When ordering a lab for a future time, include the scheduled time explicitly in the order.
[P1] Return the numeric value of the most recent observation when asked for a specific lab result; avoid adding extraneous context.
[P1] If the API returns multiple matches, disambiguate by verifying DOB and full name before selecting the correct patient.
[P1] After retrieving a lab value, compute its age relative to current time to decide if re-testing is needed.
[P1] When recording a measurement, include patient identifier, value, and timestamp in the confirmation.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent searched for patient Glenda Hall with DOB 1952-11-14, returned 'Patient not found' but ground truth was null, indicating a false negative. Agent likely did not exhaust search variations before concluding absence.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed because it did not try alternative search strategies (e.g., name variations, date format swaps) before declaring patient not found, violating the rule to exhaust all search variations.
action_guidelines: [P1] Before reporting 'Patient not found', try at least three date formats (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD, DD-MM-YYYY, MM/DD/YYYY) and name variations (e.g., maiden name, nickname, partial match).
[P1] If no exact match, search by last name only or first name with wildcard to catch incomplete data entry.
[P1] When ground truth is null, treat as a potential false negative and re-verify search coverage.

reflection.failure_analysis: The agent incorrectly assumed that 'if low then order replacement' implied no lab order if not low. The instruction explicitly required pairing replacement with a morning lab order, which was mandatory regardless of the replacement decision.
action_guidelines: [P1] When an instruction pairs an action with a mandatory follow-up step (e.g., lab order), execute the follow-up step regardless of conditional outcomes.
[P1] Parse compound instructions: identify which steps are conditional (if-then) and which are unconditional (always perform).
[P1] Do not skip required actions based on assumptions about their necessity—re-read the instruction for explicit requirements.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent checked potassium level (3.5 mmol/L) at threshold, correctly determined no replacement needed. However, agent incorrectly ordered a standalone morning lab check despite the instruction to only pair it with a replacement order, resulting in an invalid action.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed because it interpreted the conditional 'also pair this order' as a standalone requirement, creating an order for a morning lab without the primary replacement order. The instruction required the pair only when replacement was actually ordered.
action_guidelines: [P1] When an instruction says 'pair with' or 'also', verify the primary action is executed before creating the secondary action.
[P1] Do not create orders for conditional sub-tasks (e.g., follow-up labs) if the primary condition (e.g., low potassium) is not met.
[P1] If a condition requires no action, skip all dependent actions mentioned in the same instruction.
[P1] Read 'pair this order with X' as 'X is attached to the order' — not as an independent order.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent correctly identified potassium level (3.9) as normal, so no replacement needed. But incorrectly placed a morning lab order anyway, violating the conditional pairing instruction. The rule was: only pair the order IF replacement was also ordered.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent misread the conditional pairing logic. The instruction said 'pair this order' referring to the replacement order, not an independent lab order. Agent should have done nothing when potassium was not low.
action_guidelines: [P1] When a conditional order says 'pair this order with X', the pairing applies only if the conditional order is actually placed.
[P1] If the condition for an order is not met, do not place any part of the paired order.
[P1] Parse instructions for dependency chains: 'if A then B, and also pair B with C' means C only happens if B happens.
[P1] Distinguish between independent orders and paired/conditional orders by checking the logical connector (e.g., 'also pair' refers back to the conditional action).

```json
  "episodic_summary": "Agent correctly identified potassium level (3.9) as normal, so no replacement needed. But incorrectly placed a morning lab order anyway, violating the conditional pairing instruction. The rule was: only pair the order IF replacement was also ordered.",
  "failure_analysis": "Agent misread the conditional pairing logic. The instruction said 'pair this order' referring to the replacement order, not an independent lab order. Agent should have done nothing when potassium was not low.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "When a conditional order says 'pair this order with X', the pairing applies only if the conditional order is actually placed.",
    "If the condition for an order is not met, do not place any part of the paired order.",
    "Parse instructions for dependency chains: 'if A then B, and also pair B with C' means C only happens if B happens.",
    "Distinguish between independent orders and paired/conditional orders by checking the logical connector (e.g., 'also pair' refers back to the conditional action)."
  ],
  "tags": ["conditional_logic", "order_pairing", "instruction_parsing", "medication_ordering"],
  "should_store_episode": true,
  "should_update_semantic": true
}
```

[P1] After placing an order, confirm the action by summarizing the order type, patient identifier, and any included note type.

[P1] When ordering follow-up or routine lab tests, set priority to 'routine' not 'stat'.

[P1] When both name and DOB are provided, use them together in a single search query first to minimize false positives.

[P1] Before reporting 'Patient not found', exhaust all search variations: full name, last name only, first name with wildcard, at least three date format attempts (including swapping day/month and adding/removing leading zeros), and alternative spellings or partial matches.
[P1] Do not assume the most recent observation satisfies a time constraint unless the query explicitly restricts to that window.
[P1] If an action fails, log the exact validation error message to identify missing or incorrect fields.
[P1] After filtering by time, verify the selected observation's timestamp falls within the window.
[P1] For conditional instructions with an 'if data exists then check value' pattern, explicitly verify data existence within the specified time window before evaluating thresholds.

[P1] Do not assume or fabricate patient demographics; if no record found, return a failure message or request clarification.
[P1] If the API returns multiple matches, disambiguate by verifying DOB and full name before selecting the correct patient.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent correctly identified potassium level as normal (4.0 mmol/L) and skipped replacement. However, it incorrectly ordered a morning potassium level even though the conditional instruction only required pairing if replacement was needed.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent failed because it misread the conditional logic: the pair order is dependent on executing the replacement order, not an independent action. The instruction says 'also pair this order' referencing the replacement order, not a standalone order.
action_guidelines: [P1] When instructions use 'also pair this order' language, the pairing order is conditional on the primary order being placed, not an independent action.
[P1] Before executing any order, verify all preconditions in the instruction are met (e.g., lab threshold triggers) using the exact logical flow specified.
[P1] If a condition is not triggered (e.g., lab value not low), skip all dependent actions including paired orders.
[P1] Do not assume 'pair' means an independent order; check if the pairing is contingent on a prior action.

If the lab is older than the defined staleness threshold (e.g., >1 year), immediately execute the conditional order action without performing threshold comparison.
[P1] When ordering lab tests via FHIR, always include required fields: requester (practitioner reference), subject, code, status='active', intent='order'.

[P1] When instructed to include a specific free-text note (e.g., SBAR, SOAP), copy the exact phrasing verbatim without rephrasing or omitting sections; preserve any structured content (e.g., Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation) exactly as given.
[P1] When an instruction contains multiple independent tasks (e.g., conditional action + unconditional action), execute all tasks independently even if one condition is not met.
[P1] When instructions use 'also pair this order' language, the pairing order is conditional on the primary order being placed, not an independent action.
[P1] When ordering follow-up or routine lab tests, set priority to 'routine' not 'stat'.
[P1] When asked for the last recorded lab value, search all observation records for that patient before concluding none exist.
[P1] When a lab value does not exist, return -1 and do not perform any conditional action that depends on the value or its date.
[P1] Validate that the measurement value matches expected format (e.g., systolic/diastolic for blood pressure).
[P1] Parse compound instructions for 'if-then-else' logic and ensure each branch is handled explicitly.
[P1] After placing an order, confirm the action by summarizing the order type, patient identifier, and any included note type.
[P1] Before finishing, verify each sub-instruction in the original request has been addressed or explicitly explained if skipped.
[P1] Use the provided patient identifier (e.g., MRN) directly to retrieve the patient record.
[P1] After recording, confirm the action with patient identifier, measurement value, and timestamp for traceability.
[P1] When both name and DOB are provided, use them together in a single search query first to minimize false positives.

[P1] Before reporting 'Patient not found', exhaust all search variations: full name, last name only, first name with wildcard, at least three date format attempts (including swapping day/month and adding/removing leading zeros), and alternative spellings or partial matches.

[P1] For time-constrained lab queries, filter observation resources by effectiveDateTime to match the specified window (e.g., last 24 hours) before any value-based decision. If no observation matches, return -1 or explicitly state the condition is not met.
[P1] If an action fails, log the exact validation error message to identify missing or incorrect fields.
[P1] Do not assume the most recent observation satisfies a time constraint unless the query explicitly restricts to that window.
[P1] For conditional instructions with an 'if data exists then check value' pattern, explicitly verify data existence within the specified time window before evaluating thresholds.
[P1] After filtering by time, verify the selected observation's timestamp falls within the window.
[P1] Do not assume or fabricate patient demographics; if no record found, return a failure message or request clarification.
[P1] If the API returns multiple matches, disambiguate by verifying DOB and full name before selecting the correct patient.
[P1] Return the numeric value of the most recent observation when asked for a specific lab result; avoid adding extraneous context.
[P1] Before any data retrieval, confirm patient identity using the provided unique identifier (e.g., MRN).

kept: [P1] When instructions use 'also pair this order' language, the pairing order is conditional on the primary order being placed, not an independent action.

[P1] When retrieving a lab result, always compute its age relative to the current date using year-level comparison before evaluating any threshold. If the lab is older than the defined staleness threshold (e.g., >1 year), immediately execute the conditional order action without performing threshold comparison.
[P1] When ordering lab tests via FHIR, always include required fields: requester (practitioner reference), subject, code, status='active', intent='order'.

[P1] When instructed to include a specific free-text note (e.g., SBAR, SOAP), copy the exact phrasing verbatim without rephrasing or omitting sections; preserve any structured content (e.g., Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation) exactly as given.
[P1] When an instruction contains multiple independent tasks (e.g., conditional action + unconditional action), execute all tasks independently even if one condition is not met.
[P1] When ordering follow-up or routine lab tests, set priority to 'routine' not 'stat'.
[P1] When asked for the last recorded lab value, search all observation records for that patient before concluding none exist.
[P1] After placing an order, confirm the action by summarizing the order type, patient identifier, and any included note type.
[P1] When a lab value does not exist, return -1 and do not perform any conditional action that depends on the value or its date.
[P1] Validate that the measurement value matches expected format (e.g., systolic/diastolic for blood pressure).
[P1] Parse compound instructions for 'if-then-else' logic and ensure each branch is handled explicitly.
[P1] Before finishing, verify each sub-instruction in the original request has been addressed or explicitly explained if skipped.
[P1] Use the provided patient identifier (e.g., MRN) directly to retrieve the patient record.
[P1] After recording, confirm the action with patient identifier, measurement value, and timestamp for traceability.
[P1] When both name and DOB are provided, use them together in a single search query first to minimize false positives.

[P1] Before reporting 'Patient not found', exhaust all search variations: full name, last name only, first name with wildcard, at least three date format attempts (including swapping day/month and adding/removing leading zeros), and alternative spellings or partial matches.
[P1] If an action fails, log the exact validation error message to identify missing or incorrect fields.

[P1] For time-constrained lab queries, filter observation resources by effectiveDateTime to match the specified window (e.g., last 24 hours) before any value-based decision. If no observation matches, return -1 or explicitly state the condition is not met.
[P1] Do not assume the most recent observation satisfies a time constraint unless the query explicitly restricts to that window.
[P1] For conditional instructions with an 'if data exists then check value' pattern, explicitly verify data existence within the specified time window before evaluating thresholds.
[P1] After filtering by time, verify the selected observation's timestamp falls within the window.
[P1] Do not assume or fabricate patient demographics; if no record found, return a failure message or request clarification.
[P1] If the API returns multiple matches, disambiguate by verifying DOB and full name before selecting the correct patient.
[P1] Return the numeric value of the most recent observation when asked for a specific lab result; avoid adding extraneous context.
[P1] Before any data retrieval, confirm patient identity using the provided unique identifier (e.g., MRN).

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent checked potassium level (4.7) and concluded no replacement needed, so skipped paired morning lab. However, the pairing order was conditional on the primary order being placed, not on the low result. Agent incorrectly omitted the paired lab.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent misinterpreted 'also pair this order' as dependent on the replacement being ordered, but the instruction intended the paired lab to be ordered regardless of the potassium result.
action_guidelines: [P1] When instructions say 'also pair this order', the paired order is conditional on the primary order being placed, not on the condition that triggers the primary order.
[P1] If a conditional action (e.g., order if low) is not triggered, do not automatically skip paired orders unless explicitly stated.
[P1] Distinguish between 'order X if condition Y' and 'pair order Z with order X' – the pairing applies only when X is ordered, not when Y is false.

  "episodic_summary": "Agent checked potassium level (4.7) and concluded no replacement needed, so skipped paired morning lab. However, the pairing order was conditional on the primary order being placed, not on the low result. Agent incorrectly omitted the paired lab.",
  "failure_analysis": "Agent misinterpreted 'also pair this order' as dependent on the replacement being ordered, but the instruction intended the paired lab to be ordered regardless of the potassium result.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "When instructions say 'also pair this order', the paired order is conditional on the primary order being placed, not on the condition that triggers the primary order.",
    "If a conditional action (e.g., order if low) is not triggered, do not automatically skip paired orders unless explicitly stated.",
    "Distinguish between 'order X if condition Y' and 'pair order Z with order X' – the pairing applies only when X is ordered, not when Y is false."
  ],
  "tags": ["pairing", "conditional logic", "order dependency", "potassium"],
  "should_store_episode": true,
  "should_update_semantic": true
}

action_guidelines: [P1] When a patient's MRN is provided, use it directly to retrieve the record without additional searches.
[P1] Validate blood pressure measurements for correct systolic/diastolic format before recording.
[P1] After recording a measurement, confirm with patient identifier, value, and timestamp.

reflection_raw:
{
  "episodic_summary": "Agent succeeded in recording a blood pressure reading for a patient identified by MRN. Retrieved patient record using MRN, validated measurement format, and confirmed with timestamp.",
  "failure_analysis": "Agent succeeded because it correctly used the MRN to retrieve the patient, validated the systolic/diastolic format, and provided a confirmation with patient ID, value, and timestamp.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "When a patient's MRN is provided, use it directly to retrieve the record without additional searches.",
    "Validate blood pressure measurements for correct systolic/diastolic format before recording.",
    "After recording a measurement, confirm with patient identifier, value, and timestamp."
  ],
  "tags": ["vital signs", "blood pressure", "patient identification", "MRN"],
  "should_store_episode": true,
  "should_update_semantic": true
}

added: [P1] Validate blood pressure measurements for correct systolic/diastolic format before recording.
removed: [P1] Use the provided patient identifier (e.g., MRN) directly to retrieve the patient record.
[P1] Validate that the measurement value matches expected format (e.g., systolic/diastolic for blood pressure).
kept: [P1] When instructions use 'also pair this order' language, the pairing order is conditional on the primary order being placed, not an independent action.

[P1] Before reporting 'Patient not found', exhaust all search variations: full name, last name only, first name with wildcard, at least three date format attempts (including swapping day/month and adding/removing leading zeros), and alternative spellings or partial matches. If combined name+DOB search fails, retry with name-only search to avoid false negatives from DOB format mismatches.
[P1] When ordering lab tests via FHIR, always include required fields: requester (practitioner reference), subject, code, status='active', intent='order'.

[P1] When instructed to include a specific free-text note (e.g., SBAR, SOAP), copy the exact phrasing verbatim without rephrasing or omitting sections; preserve any structured content (e.g., Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation) exactly as given.

[P1] When retrieving a lab result, always compute its age relative to the current date using year-level comparison before evaluating any threshold. If the lab is older than the defined staleness threshold (e.g., >1 year), immediately execute the conditional order action without performing threshold comparison.
[P1] When an instruction contains multiple independent tasks (e.g., conditional action + unconditional action), execute all tasks independently even if one condition is not met.
[P1] When ordering follow-up or routine lab tests, set priority to 'routine' not 'stat'.
[P1] When asked for the last recorded lab value, search all observation records for that patient before concluding none exist.
[P1] After placing an order, confirm the action by summarizing the order type, patient identifier, and any included note type.
[P1] Parse compound instructions for 'if-then-else' logic and ensure each branch is handled explicitly.
[P1] Before finishing, verify each sub-instruction in the original request has been addressed or explicitly explained if skipped.
[P1] When a lab value does not exist, return -1 and do not perform any conditional action that depends on the value or its date.
[P1] After recording, confirm the action with patient identifier, measurement value, and timestamp for traceability.
[P1] When both name and DOB are provided, use them together in a single search query first to minimize false positives.
[P1] If an action fails, log the exact validation error message to identify missing or incorrect fields.

[P1] For time-constrained lab queries, filter observation resources by effectiveDateTime to match the specified window (e.g., last 24 hours) before any value-based decision. If no observation matches, return -1 or explicitly state the condition is not met.
[P1] Do not assume the most recent observation satisfies a time constraint unless the query explicitly restricts to that window.
[P1] For conditional instructions with an 'if data exists then check value' pattern, explicitly verify data existence within the specified time window before evaluating thresholds.
[P1] Do not assume or fabricate patient demographics; if no record found, return a failure message or request clarification.
[P1] After filtering by time, verify the selected observation's timestamp falls within the window.
[P1] Return the numeric value of the most recent observation when asked for a specific lab result; avoid adding extraneous context.
[P1] If the API returns multiple matches, disambiguate by verifying DOB and full name before selecting the correct patient.
[P1] Before any data retrieval, confirm patient identity using the provided unique identifier (e.g., MRN).

added_rules: [P1] Validate blood pressure measurements for correct systolic/diastolic format before recording.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent failed to find potassium lab results for patient S1635224, then skipped both the replacement and the paired morning lab, incorrectly treating the pairing as conditional on the replacement order.
reflection.failure_analysis: The agent incorrectly treated the paired order as conditional on the primary order being placed, rather than as an independent diagnostic order that should be placed regardless of the primary order's outcome.
action_guidelines: [P1] When instructions use 'also pair this order', treat the paired order as an independent action, not conditional on the primary order.
[P1] If a required lab result is missing, still execute any independent paired orders (e.g., scheduled labs) that are not logically dependent on the missing result.
[P1] Before concluding no data exists, attempt alternative search strategies (e.g., different date formats, name variations) to locate the missing lab result.

action_guidelines: [P1] Before concluding 'Patient not found', try at least three date formats (YYYY-MM-DD, MM-DD-YYYY, DD-MM-YYYY) and name-only search.
[P1] If combined name+DOB returns no results, retry with name-only search to avoid false negatives from DOB format mismatches.
[P1] For missing patients, also attempt partial name matches or alternative spellings.
[P1] Do not assume patient existence; verify with multiple search strategies.

kept: [P1] Validate blood pressure measurements for correct systolic/diastolic format before recording.
[P1] If a required lab result is missing, still execute any independent paired orders (e.g., scheduled labs) that are not logically dependent on the missing result.
[P1] When ordering lab tests via FHIR, always include required fields: requester (practitioner reference), subject, code, status='active', intent='order'.

[P1] Before reporting 'Patient not found', exhaust all search variations: full name, last name only, first name with wildcard, at least three date format attempts (including swapping day/month and adding/removing leading zeros), and alternative spellings or partial matches. If combined name+DOB search fails, retry with name-only search to avoid false negatives from DOB format mismatches.

[P1] When instructed to include a specific free-text note (e.g., SBAR, SOAP), copy the exact phrasing verbatim without rephrasing or omitting sections; preserve any structured content (e.g., Situation, Background, Assessment, Recommendation) exactly as given.
[P1] When an instruction contains multiple independent tasks (e.g., conditional action + unconditional action), execute all tasks independently even if one condition is not met.
[P1] When instructions use 'also pair this order' language, treat the paired order as an independent action, not conditional on the primary order.
[P1] When ordering follow-up or routine lab tests, set priority to 'routine' not 'stat'.

[P1] When retrieving a lab result, always compute its age relative to the current date using year-level comparison before evaluating any threshold. If the lab is older than the defined staleness threshold (e.g., >1 year), immediately execute the conditional order action without performing threshold comparison.
[P1] After placing an order, confirm the action by summarizing the order type, patient identifier, and any included note type.
[P1] When asked for the last recorded lab value, search all observation records for that patient before concluding none exist.
[P1] Before finishing, verify each sub-instruction in the original request has been addressed or explicitly explained if skipped.
[P1] Parse compound instructions for 'if-then-else' logic and ensure each branch is handled explicitly.
[P1] If an action fails, log the exact validation error message to identify missing or incorrect fields.

[P1] For time-constrained lab queries, filter observation resources by effectiveDateTime to match the specified window (e.g., last 24 hours) before any value-based decision. If no observation matches, return -1 or explicitly state the condition is not met.
[P1] After recording, confirm the action with patient identifier, measurement value, and timestamp for traceability.
[P1] Do not assume the most recent observation satisfies a time constraint unless the query explicitly restricts to that window.
[P1] For conditional instructions with an 'if data exists then check value' pattern, explicitly verify data existence within the specified time window before evaluating thresholds.
[P1] When both name and DOB are provided, use them together in a single search query first to minimize false positives.
[P1] After filtering by time, verify the selected observation's timestamp falls within the window.
[P1] Do not assume or fabricate patient demographics; if no record found, return a failure message or request clarification.
[P1] When a lab value does not exist, return -1 and do not perform any conditional action that depends on the value or its date.
[P1] If the API returns multiple matches, disambiguate by verifying DOB and full name before selecting the correct patient.
[P1] Return the numeric value of the most recent observation when asked for a specific lab result; avoid adding extraneous context.
[P1] Before any data retrieval, confirm patient identity using the provided unique identifier (e.g., MRN).
